sglang.title: [Bug] Forward fixed_split_size in SWA / cross-attention paths of FlashInfer backend

Bug
--enable-deterministic-inference sets prefill_split_tile_size — the FlashInfer knob that pins split-K reduction order so attention output is batch-invariant — and threads it through the update() chain as fixed_split_size (plus disable_split_kv on the decode side). But three of the four update_*

sglang.title: Fix FA DRAFT_EXTEND_V2 cache extent
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
FA's init_forward_metadata and init_forward_metadata_replay_cuda_graph produced incorrect cache-extent metadata for DRAFT_EXTEND_V2, causing the FlashAttention kernel to read stale memory beyond the prefix boundary. End-to-end this surfaced as ~0.55 max absolute difference vs an HF-style

sglang.title: Expose Flex attention causal/decode masks as static methods
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
torch.nn.attention.flex_attention.create_block_mask expects a plain callable with the signature (b, h, q_idx, kv_idx) -> bool. When the mask is passed as a bound method (e.g. self._causal_mask), the closure captured by create_block_mask includes a reference to the TorchFlexAttnBackend ins

sglang.title: Allow Optional key/value in unified_attention_with_output split-op (MLA absorb fix)
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
unified_attention_with_output is the split-op entry point used by the piecewise / breakable CUDA graph runner (PCG/BCG) to dispatch through the active attention backend. MLA's absorb path calls RadixAttention.forward(q, k=None, v=None, ...) because compressed latent KV is read from the to

RMSNorm.forward_cuda silently drops the cast_x_before_out_mul flag when a residual is provided.

This PR int

sglang.title: fix: copy seq_lens in TRTLLM MHA draft decode cuda graph capture
sglang.author: libertyeagle
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
TRTLLMHAAttnBackend.init_forward_metadata_capture_cuda_graph re-uses a
pre-allocated, zero-initialized cache_seqlens buffer for the draft-decode
branch but never copies the current seq_lens into it. As a result, the
captured kernel runs with cache_seqlens == 0 (empty KV range), which
prod

vllm.title: Restore `Literal` for `WeightTransferConfig.backend`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Using Literal[...] | str is the pattern used elswhere to indicate that the Literal contains the in-tree options and str allows any value to be passed for out-of-tree options.
The Literal was removed in #43121 and this PR adds it back so that the API docs linked to by the CLI help is more useful.

vllm.title: [XPU] add scale transpose to prepare_fp8_moe_layer_for_xpu and bump up kernels
vllm.author: mayuyuace
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Block fp8 has scales which also need be transposed.

vllm.title: [kv_offload] Skip decode-phase blocks in CPU offload
vllm.author: Etelis
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reasoning models strip prior <think> across turns. A completed turn's
decode KV is therefore reuse-dead: the thinking is dropped, and the answer is
re-prefilled at a shifted position next turn (new block hashes). Offloading
those blocks GPU→CPU burns PCIe bandwidth and evicts genuinely reusable pref

vllm.author: yintong-lu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Gemma4-26B-A3B failed to run on Intel XPU with current vllm-xpu-kernels  and vllm  , after the Gemma4 MoE activation in vLLM has been changed from gelu to gelu_tanh  by PR in Gemma4MoE.init().  This breaks Gemma4 serving on Intel XPU because the XPU MoE backend does not wire gelu_tanh throu
